summary: The longest chapter in the Bible — an elaborate alphabetic acrostic meditating on the beauty, power, and life-giving sufficiency of God's Word across all of life's circumstances.
psalm_rows_html:
TypeAuthorSituationKey ThemeNotable VerseNT Connection
Torah/Wisdom (Acrostic)Unknown (possibly Ezra or a post-exilic scribe)General meditation; the author faces enemies, affliction, and longing for God's deliveranceThe perfection, joy, and life-giving power of God's Word (Torah)v. 105 — "Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my path"John 1:1-14 (Jesus as the Word incarnate); Heb 4:12 (the Word is living and active); Rom 7:22 (delight in God's law)
